paper_title: Measurement of the physico-chemical parameters of sediment samples

methods.description: Measurement of pH and temperature of sediment samples at the time of sampling using specific instruments.

steps.description: The pH was measured using a pH meter (HACH Sension3 Company, Loveland, Colo, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH using HACH Sension3 pH meter

steps.description: Temperature was measured using a liquid thermometer (GH, ZEAL LTD, London, England) at the time of sediment sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: measure temperature using GH ZEAL LTD liquid thermometer

methods.description: Determination of the percentages of sand, silt, and clay in sediment samples using a sedimentation method.

steps.description: About 100 g of sediment was homogenized through a less than 2 mm sieve (ASTEAE-11, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment sample to less than 2 mm

steps.description: A straight-sided glass bottle was filled to about 1/3 full with sieved sediments. Distilled water was added to fill about 3/4 of bottle, and the bottle was capped and shaken vigorously for 10 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: mix sediment with water in a bottle and shake

steps.description: The bottle was placed on a desk, where it was undisturbed for several weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: allow sediment to settle undisturbed

steps.description: After 1-2 minutes, a first reading was taken with a wax pencil marking the level of sediment in the bottle; this represented the sand.
steps.searchable_summary: mark sand level in sediment bottle

steps.description: A second reading was noted after 2 hours between 2 lines, which indicated the silt.
steps.searchable_summary: mark silt level in sediment bottle

steps.description: After 2 weeks, a third reading was taken at the bottom of the bottle, which denoted the clay.
steps.searchable_summary: mark clay level in sediment bottle

steps.description: The noted levels of sand, silt and clay were divided by the total level of sediment and multiplied by 100 to give the percentages of sand, silt and clay.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentages of sand, silt, and clay

methods.description: Procedure to measure the carbon content in sediment samples by combustion.

steps.description: About 10 g of sediment sample was sieved and ground with a pestle.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve and grind sediment sample

steps.description: A crucible was used to dry this sediment sample at 60 C.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sediment sample in crucible at 60 C

steps.description: A new crucible was weighed and denoted as m_cruc.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh empty crucible

steps.description: About 1 g of the dried sediment sample was placed in this crucible and weighed and denoted as m_initial.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh crucible with sediment

steps.description: This crucible was put into a furnace at 1000 C overnight for the destruction of the carbon content.
steps.searchable_summary: combust sediment in furnace at 1000 C

steps.description: Then the crucible was cooled and weighed and denoted as m_final.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh crucible after combustion

methods.description: Procedure to determine the water content in sediment samples by drying.

steps.description: An evaporating dish was washed, placed into a drying oven at 105 C for 30 minutes, cooled to room temperature in a desiccator and weighed (m_a).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and weigh evaporating dish

steps.description: Then 0.5 g of sediment was put into the evaporating dish and again its weight was measured (m_b).
steps.searchable_summary: weigh dish with sediment

steps.description: This evaporating dish with sediment was placed into a drying oven at 105 C overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sediment in oven at 105 C

steps.description: The evaporating dish was cooled again to room temperature in the closed desiccator and weighed (m_c).
steps.searchable_summary: weigh dish after drying

methods.description: Measurement of the electrical conductivity of sediment samples using an EC meter.

steps.description: The probes of the EC meter were firstly calibrated with standard solutions (with known conductivity).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate EC meter with standard solutions

steps.description: The sediment sample was put into an SMFC, then the probe tips of the EC meter were inserted into the sediment; after waiting until the reading on the EC meter was stabilized, this reading was noted as the electrical conductivity.
steps.searchable_summary: measure electrical conductivity of sediment
